name: tytorch

metadata.name: tytorch
metadata.summary: Based on a fork of the amazing mltrainer and mads_datasets by R. Grouls, tytorch is an opinionated base for working with data for and training with pytorch.

documentation:
Based on a fork of the amazing mltrainer and mads_datasets by R. Grouls, 
tytorch is an opinionated base for working with data for and training with pytorch.

The Dataset and DataPipeline follows a strategy pattern that provides for a relatively flat class hierarchy while at the same time allowing for maximum code reuse

The Trainer assumes mlflow for tracking and ray for tuning and is compatible with all the pytorch native dataloaders and objects, as well as the mads_datasets factories and datasets.

variables.name: tytorch.examples.traintune.classification.image.flowers.tune.model
documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: tytorch.examples.traintune.classification.sequence.gestures.tune.model
documentation: type: ignore

functions.name: tytorch.examples.autoencoder.train_autoencoder.select_n_random
documentation: Selects n random datapoints and their corresponding labels from a dataset

methods.name: tytorch.datapipeline.DataLoadingStrategy.load_data
documentation:
Load the dataset. This method should return a data object or None if
no data needs to be loaded at this step.
If global transformations are to be applied, they should modify the data directly.

Returns:
    data: The loaded data or None if the data is already available.

methods.name: tytorch.datapipeline.GlobalTransformStrategy.set_pipeline
documentation:
Optional method to provide access to the DataPipeline instance, allowing
the global transformation strategy to interact with the data and labels.

methods.name: tytorch.datapipeline.GlobalTransformStrategy.apply
documentation:
Apply a global transformation to the dataset.

Args:
    pipeline: The DataPipeline instance which holds the data and labels.

Returns:
    data: The transformed data or None if the transformation modifies the pipeline's data/labels in place.

methods.name: tytorch.datapipeline.ItemTransformStrategy.set_pipeline
documentation:
Optional method to provide access to the DataPipeline instance, allowing
the item transformation strategy to interact with the data or metadata if needed.

methods.name: tytorch.datapipeline.ItemTransformStrategy.apply
documentation:
Apply a transformation to an individual data sample.

Args:
    data: The individual sample to be transformed.

Returns:
    transformed_data: The transformed data sample.

methods.name: tytorch.datapipeline.DataPipeline.apply_global_transformations
documentation: Apply global transformations to the entire dataset.

methods.name: tytorch.datapipeline.DataPipeline.create_datasets
documentation:
Creates and returns three TyTorchDataset instances for train, validation, and test sets.

Returns:
    train_dataset: TyTorchDataset for the training set.
    val_dataset: TyTorchDataset for the validation set.
    test_dataset: TyTorchDataset for the test set.

methods.name: tytorch.strategies.global_transform_strategies.ImageTensorSplitStrategy.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    test_frac: Proportion of the dataset to include in the test split.
    valid_frac: Proportion of the training data to include in the validation split.

methods.name: tytorch.strategies.global_transform_strategies.ImageTensorSplitStrategy.apply
documentation: Applies the dataset split and stores the resulting datasets in the pipeline.

methods.name: tytorch.strategies.global_transform_strategies.SequenceTensorSplitStrategy.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    test_frac: Proportion of the dataset to include in the test split.
    valid_frac: Proportion of the training data to include in the validation split.

methods.name: tytorch.strategies.global_transform_strategies.SequenceTensorSplitStrategy.apply
documentation:
Applies dataset splitting for both tensor and list inputs, supporting variable-length sequences.
Handles both uniform-length tensors and variable-length sequences stored in lists.

Args:
    pipeline: Pipeline object containing data and labels

methods.name: tytorch.strategies.item_transform_strategies.ImageTensorAugmentationStrategy.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    crop_size: Tuple indicating the size to which the image will be resized and cropped.
    rotation_degrees: Tuple indicating the range of degrees for random rotation.

methods.name: tytorch.strategies.item_transform_strategies.ImageTensorAugmentationStrategy.apply
documentation:
Apply a series of random augmentations to the input tensor.

Args:
    x: Input tensor representing the image.

Returns:
    Transformed tensor.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.trainer_utils.save_params_to_disk
documentation: Save the params dictionary (with class references) to a pickle file on disk.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.trainer_utils.load_params_from_disk
documentation: Load the params dictionary (with class references) from a pickle file on disk.

methods.name: tytorch.trainer.EarlyStopping.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    patience (int): How many epochs to wait after last improvement.
    min_delta (float): Minimum change to qualify as an improvement.
    mode (str): 'min' for minimizing loss, 'max' for maximizing metrics like accuracy.

methods.name: tytorch.trainer.EarlyStopping.save_checkpoint
documentation: Saves model when validation loss decrease.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.mlflow.set_best_run_tag_and_log_model
documentation:
Finds the run with the best metric result in an MLflow experiment (by name), sets a tag 'best_run=True',
and logs a model to that run.

Parameters:
- experiment_name (str): The name of the MLflow experiment.
- metric_name (str): The name of the metric to evaluate.
- model_path (str): Path to the PyTorch model (.pth file) to log to the best run.
- model_class (torch.nn.Module): The model class to load the saved state_dict into.
- direction (str): The optimization direction, either 'min' or 'max'. Defaults to 'max'.

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the MASE metric with training data for scaling.
Args:
    train (BaseDatastreamer): Training data used to compute the scaling factor.
    horizon (int): Forecasting horizon.

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.naivenorm
documentation: Compute the scaling factor using naive predictions on the training data.

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.naivepredict
documentation: Generate naive predictions using the last available values.

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.mae
documentation: Compute Mean Absolute Error (MAE).

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.update
documentation: Update internal state with new predictions and ground truths.

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.compute
documentation: Compute the final MASE value.

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.reset
documentation: Reset internal state.

methods.name: tytorch.metrics.MASE.merge_state
documentation:
Merge states from multiple metrics for distributed computing.

Args:
    metrics: List of MASE metrics to merge from

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.add_batch_padding
documentation:
Pads a batch of sequences and converts labels to a tensor.

Args:
    batch: A list of tuples where each tuple contains a tensor (sequence) and an integer label.

Returns:
    A tuple containing:
    - A tensor of padded sequences.
    - A tensor of labels.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.get_file
documentation:
Download a file from url to location data_dir / filename

Args:
    data_dir (Path): dir to store file
    filename (Path): filename
    url (str): url to obtain filename
    unzip (bool, optional): If the file needs unzipping
    overwrite (bool, optional): overwrite file, if it already exists.

Returns:
    Path: The path of the downloaded file

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.download_data
documentation:
Standalone function to handle the extraction and return the path to the downloaded file.

Args:
    source_url (str): URL to download the data
    bronze_folder (str): Folder to save the downloaded file
    bronze_filename (str): Filename to save the file as
    unzip (bool): Whether to unzip the file after downloading
    overwrite (bool): Whether to overwrite the file if it already exists

Returns:
    Path: Path of the downloaded file, or None if the file already exists and overwrite is False

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.custom_split
documentation:
Custom dataset split function that handles pandas DataFrame, Polars DataFrame,
NumPy array, and PyTorch tensor, splitting them into train, validation, and test sets.

Args:
    data: The dataset (pandas DataFrame, Polars DataFrame, NumPy array, or PyTorch tensor).
    labels: Corresponding labels.
    test_size: Proportion of the data to be used as the test set.
    val_size: Proportion of the remaining data to be used as the validation set.
    random_state: Seed for reproducibility.

Returns:
    Tuple containing train, validation, and test splits of data and labels.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.split_train_val_direct
documentation:
Splits the training data and labels directly without creating an intermediate dataset.

Args:
    data (dict): The original data loaded from the .pt file.
    val_fraction (float): Fraction of the data to use for validation.

Returns:
    new_data (dict): New dataset containing training, validation, and test data and labels.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.walk_dir
documentation:
loops recursively through a folder

Args:
    path (Path): folder to loop through. If a directory
        is encountered, loop through that recursively.

Yields:
    Generator: all paths in a folder and subdirs.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.iter_valid_paths
documentation:
Gets all paths in folders and subfolders
strips the classnames assuming that the subfolders are the classnames
Keeps only paths with the right suffix


Args:
    path (Path): image folder
    formats (List[str]): suffices to keep, eg [".jpg", ".png"]

Returns:
    Tuple[Iterator[Path], List[str]]: An iterator of paths and a list of class names

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.pad_collate_packed
documentation:
Custom collate function to pad sequences in a batch.

Args:
    batch (list): List of (sequence, label) tuples.

Returns:
    Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]: Padded sequences and corresponding labels.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.pad_collate
documentation:
Custom collate function to pad sequences in a batch.

Args:
    batch (list): List of (sequence, label) tuples.

Returns:
    Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]: Padded sequences and corresponding labels.

functions.name: tytorch.utils.data.ensure_3d_tensor
documentation:
Ensures the input tensor is 3D (batch_size, sequence_length, features).
If input is 2D, assumes it's (batch_size, sequence_length) and adds a feature dimension.

Args:
    data: Input tensor that's either 2D or 3D
    feature_dim: Size of feature dimension if we need to add it (default: 1)
    
Returns:
    3D tensor with shape (batch_size, sequence_length, features)
